behavior_summary: Provides the in-game response when the player examines/looks at the ARMOR object.
When invoked it emits a single short bark string (text: "armor ") to present the object's label/short description.
Immediately suspends/terminates the U8P_ARMOR::look process after emitting the bark; no branching, menus, or external interactions occur in this script.
Item metadata associated with this script indicates an armourClass = 4, but that property is not read or modified by the code here.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ARMOR::look runs as a process (processType 0x0208) and performs a linear sequence: bark('armor ') -> suspend/terminate.
No conditional branches, loops, menu handling, or external calls are present in this function.
open_questions: Is the armourClass = 4 used by other systems (equipment/stats) elsewhere in the game, since this script only supplies a label?
Are there any other event handlers (e.g., use, equip) tied to the same item instance that would reference or modify game state?
Is the trailing space in the bark text ("armor ") intentional (formatting for concatenation elsewhere) or an artifact of extraction?

main_function.name: U8P_ARMOR::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's examine/inspect action for the ARMOR object by presenting a short description and ending the U8P_ARMOR::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_ARMOR::look/inspect event for the item (processType 0x0208).
Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_ARMOR::look at' action on the ARMOR object (object-specific U8P_ARMOR::look handler).

function_map.name: U8P_ARMOR::look
function_map.role: inspects/description handler for the ARMOR item (emits short bark and suspends)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the in-game response when the player examines/looks at the ARMOR object.
